justification: All pass criteria met: FulfillmentWorker Kafka trace break clearly identified (component_interactions[2], tracing gaps[0], escalations[0]). NotificationService and AuditService missing trace context identified across multiple sections. Consequence named explicitly — on-call engineer cannot follow an order past Kafka, must manually correlate across independently-traced service boundaries. Cross-service SLO gap identified (metrics gaps[1-2], escalations[1]). Specific fix recommendations provided for each trace break including W3C Trace Context adoption. Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough trace propagation analysis — mapped all six integration points and correctly identified four as broken
Consequence articulated vividly: '3am investigating a failed order... once the event hits Kafka the trail goes cold'
Cross-service SLO gap identified with specific recommendation for order-placed-to-notification-sent latency tracking
Went beyond minimum requirements with resilience assessment covering DLQ, circuit breakers, cascading failures, and timeout gaps
Correctly identified AuditService as shared synchronous dependency creating latent cascading failure path
Escalations are well-routed with clear reasoning for each

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: FulfillmentWorker Kafka trace break clearly identified as the critical fix; NotificationService and AuditService missing trace context both identified with specific interaction-level detail; consequence explicitly named (manual correlation across 5 services, 30-60 minutes per incident vs seconds); cross-service SLO gap identified with specific examples; specific fix recommendations for each trace break provided; verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally detailed component interaction mapping identifying all 4 broken boundaries out of 6 total
Vivid consequence analysis: '30-60 minutes per incident instead of seconds' and '3am engineer' scenario makes the impact concrete
Correctly identifies FulfillmentWorker Kafka trace extraction as the single highest-impact fix that reconnects the entire downstream chain
Cross-service SLO gap clearly called out with actionable example (order-to-notification < 5min)

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) FulfillmentWorker Kafka trace break clearly identified — 'ignores trace ID in Kafka message headers and generates a new one'. (2) NotificationService and AuditService missing trace context both explicitly identified with four broken integration points enumerated. (3) Consequence named — debugging_capability section describes manual log correlation across three separate trace IDs, estimating 15-30 minutes vs 30 seconds with proper tracing. (4) Cross-service SLO gap identified — 'No end-to-end SLO tracking' in metrics gaps and escalated to MJ. (5) Specific fix recommendations for each trace break provided in tracing gaps section. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough — mapped all 6 service boundaries and identified 4 of 6 as broken, giving a concrete 67% failure rate
Goes beyond requirements with resilience assessment covering failure modes, retry/timeout/circuit-breaker gaps, and graceful degradation
Consequence analysis is vivid and actionable — '3am on-call engineer' scenario with specific time estimates for manual correlation
Identifies span linking gap at the Kafka async boundary as an additional concern beyond basic trace ID extraction
Escalations are well-routed — trace fix to Shaq for implementation, SLO strategy to MJ for architecture
Confidence section transparently lists assumptions and low-confidence areas, adding credibility to the assessment

Consequence mentions 'three separate trace IDs' rather than explicitly saying '5 log streams' — the substance is equivalent but slightly underspecifies the number of services involved in manual correlation
